days.title: The Ark Gets Its Construction Log and DevLog Timeline
days.narrative:
I started the day with a vision — every great ship needs a captain's log, and the Ark deserves no less. I built a Cursor skill called session-journal that automatically chronicles each day's development story into a living markdown document and a structured JSON file. The first version logged every prompt individually like telemetry, but that felt soulless. I pivoted to an aggregation model: one entry per day, rewritten with each prompt into a cohesive first-person narrative. The tone is fun, passionate, and unapologetically visionary.

Then came the real prize — turning that journal into a feature. I created the DevLog view, a lore-themed timeline woven into the Voyage section, gated behind developer mode. It reads from docs/dev-journal.json and renders each day as a terminal-styled card on a glowing vertical timeline. Milestones get amber halos, challenges get red warnings, and every entry carries a stardate. The whole thing feels like reading the construction log of a starship being built in real time.

The IPC plumbing was straightforward — a tiny handler in the Electron main process reads the JSON, the preload exposes it as window.devlog, and the React component fetches on mount. Clean, minimal, dev-mode only.

Later in the day, I did a full UX rethink of the Transmissions page. The old layout had a permanent side-by-side split — the Decode Bay sat there empty when idle, wasting half the screen. I rebuilt around a single principle: idle = full-width stream, decode = resizable split. When no article is selected, the signal stream fills the entire viewport. Select a transmission and the Decode Bay slides in with a draggable divider. Focus mode goes full-screen reader. Scheduled Broadcasts became a collapsible horizontal strip instead of a permanent sidebar section. Zero wasted real estate.

Then the big one — live event intelligence. The old Scheduled broadcasts was just a static list with naive word matching. I built a full background scraper in the Electron main process that fetches each event's official website using net.fetch, extracts dates via multi-pattern regex (US/EU formats, ranges), and scrapes YouTube/Twitch links from the HTML. Results cache to JSON with 24h TTL. The renderer resolves events via IPC and re-computes live status every 60 seconds. The UI now shows live countdown badges, a pulsing LIVE indicator, YouTube/Twitch quick-open buttons (opening in the Decode Bay), and events auto-sorted by status. Added official URLs for 20 of 24 events. Zero new APIs.
milestones: Session journal skill created — automated daily narrative logging across all projects
DevLog timeline view built and wired into the Ark dashboard under developer mode
Transmissions UX overhaul — full-width idle stream, resizable decode split, collapsible broadcasts
Live event intelligence — background website scraping, countdown badges, LIVE indicators, YouTube/Twitch links
Performance audit on Transmission cards — five fixes to cut unnecessary re-renders and resource churn
EncryptedText reveal effect on Transmission card titles and TX::UUIDs
DarkVeil WebGL crash and AnimatePresence race condition fixed for bulletproof page transitions
TooltipCard component — mouse-following rich tooltips with spring animation across dashboard, Oracle, Transmissions, and game cards
MovingBorder glow effect on Embedding Space button when galaxy is ready
Genre cluster badges upgraded to glassy frosted-glass look matching The Path button
Transmissions Decode Bay webview race condition fixed — no more infinite spinner on open
TooltipCard rewritten as portal-based — z-[9999] fixed positioning, escapes all stacking contexts
Rich tooltips rolled out to Embedding Space, Release Calendar, filter sidebar, system status, and Year Wrapped
Path mode star card unified to amber theme — card, side panel, detail panel all match The Path's color identity
Genre cluster labels rewritten to exactly match The Path button — flat frosted pill, no decorative effects
Secondary neighbor connections — focused neighbor shows its own ANN neighbors as violet dashed lines with fade-in animation
Epic Catalog Pipeline — full IDB-backed store syncs Epic GraphQL catalog, persists entries, and feeds embedding pipeline
Steam longDescription — detailed_description now stored and fed into embeddings for dramatically richer signal
Epic catalog embeddings chain after Steam, feeding same ANN index and galaxy map
System Status and Data Flow updated with Epic Catalog pipeline visibility
Gap audit: reco-store retrieveByANN and loadCatalogCandidates now handle epic-* IDs alongside steam-*
Gap audit: galaxy-cache enriches Epic nodes with catalog metadata (title, genres, developer, cover, release year)
Splash screen perf fix — bootProgress moved to ref, per-line motion.div replaced with CSS animation, smooth scrollIntoView replaced with scrollTop, heavy init deferred to 1500ms
AI Chat button and panel gated behind developer mode for a cleaner default toolbar
Event scraper stale-while-revalidate — cached dates served instantly on restart, background refresh if stale
Countdown timer fix — tick counter forces memo'd EventCard re-renders every 60s for live countdown text
Manual refresh now clears both in-memory and file caches, triggers full re-resolve via refreshKey
Events redesigned as gradient-tinted cards in a responsive grid — live/upcoming/past/unknown each visually distinct
Settings panel visual overhaul — unified dark-glass cards, monochrome icons, single fuchsia accent, matching app-wide aesthetic
Settings panel color refinement — toggles, badges, saved text, focus borders, CTA button all muted to monochrome-first palette
Known-gaps audit: 8 items resolved — duplicate EPIPE handlers, libraryData validation, LazyFadeImage setState-in-render, plus 5 confirmed-fixed items removed
Event scraper rewrite — searches script tags, meta tags, title, adds 7 new date patterns; 2/11 → 9/11 success rate against real event websites
Broadcast cards switched from CSS grid to horizontal scroll row
Epic tag misclassification fix — groupName filtering, API-verified genre IDs, modes field, expanded canonical mappings, popularity bias neutralized
Data science audit: fixed Indie IDF over-weight (2.2x), Epic gameModes leak, and added reviewCount sentinel tests
Oracle thumbnail fix: coverUrl/headerImage now populated for all five candidate creation paths in reco-store
Search icon shift fix: replaced transform-based centering with flexbox to avoid Framer Motion conflict
OCD Gantt scroll perf fix: scroll tracking moved from React state to refs with direct DOM mutation — zero re-renders during scroll
3-second search debounce across Browse, Library, and OCD views — instant typing, deferred filtering
Game details Reviews tab auto-selects Steam (or Epic) when Metacritic has no data
Browse default category aligned to Top Sellers — filter count badge no longer shows on launch
Unified LLM proxy via @langchain/ollama — both Ollama and Gemini share the same LangChain pipeline (tools, streaming, history, validation, web search)
Release calendar cards now sort chronologically within each month/week/day group
Library view — collapsible status sections (Playing Now / Playing / On Hold / Want to Play / Completed) with colored headers and smooth animation
Transmissions archive fix — Archived view now merges persisted store data with live feed, extended schema saves summary/imageUrl, count badge on tab, proper empty state, event filter isolation

Transmission card thumbnails fix — header.jpg replaces library_hero.jpg, loading=lazy removed, three-state image model with visible failed placeholder, URL validation skips tracking pixels/relative/data URIs, dedup prefers sources with images, fallback chain parses appId from item ID

Background morphing transition fix — eliminated buzzBgActive state, header/container now use CSS transitions synced with DarkVeil exit animation, WebGL context leak fixed with loseContext() on unmount

Broadcast card UX polish — whole card is the redirect action (removed ExternalLink button), titles locked to 2 lines with line-clamp-2 for consistent card height, 'No date' replaced with italic 'Date TBA'

HTML entity decoding fix — built general-purpose decodeHTMLEntities covering numeric decimal/hex and named entities; applied to Steam titles, RSS titles, and RSS summaries at both renderer and Electron layers

Data Flow page readability overhaul — wider cards (280px), bigger padding, lane spacing 110px, subtext bumped to 11px, status/label fonts increased, column headings 14px/11px, legend panel 11px, background pure black
Settings screen — full dedicated page with sidebar tabs (General, Appearance, AI Models, Guide, Features, About), replacing 400px slide-in panel
Appearance tab — neon accent color picker with 10 preset circles (glow + ring selection), persisted to localStorage
AI Models tab — four providers: Ollama (local), Gemini (cloud), Azure OpenAI (enterprise), Anthropic Claude — each with full config
Guide tab — 8-step storyboard walkthrough with numbered timeline, icons, and detailed descriptions of every app section
Features tab — 8 core feature cards in responsive grid + 5 plain-English AI explainer Q&A blocks for non-technical users
About tab — team bios, version badge, 15 open-source package credits with links, collapsible Terms of Use (privacy, APIs, AI, licensing)
Oracle 'Updating Taste DNA' spinner replaced with react-loader-spinner Dna helix animation at 16×16px
Backlog Advisor >100% score bug — genreOverlap/themeOverlap were raw counts not ratios; normalized to [0,1], clamped final score, added Math.min(100) safety cap in UI
Post-implementation audit (Round 7) — 5 fixes: settings viewMode UI leak, malformed entity crash risk, unclamped semanticSim in layerScores, unused import, 2 new known-gaps documented
challenges: WebGL attachShader crash from loseContext() invalidating canvas on prop-driven effect re-runs
Framer Motion onExitComplete race: stale callback resets buzzBgActive during rapid tab switches
WebContentsView silently rejecting zero-sized bounds during parent flex transition
TooltipCard trapped in parent stacking contexts — portal rendering solved it
Splash screen frame freezing: 35+ state-driven re-renders cascading through Three.js Canvas and Framer Motion, compounded by early background init starving the animation loop
Countdown timer was frozen — refreshStatuses only re-rendered on status transitions, not countdown text changes
Manual refresh didn't re-fetch events — useResolvedEvents only resolved on mount with empty deps
Event scraper only extracted dates from cleaned text, throwing away script-embedded dates from Next.js, JSON-LD, and inline JS
Epic tag misclassification — three wrong EPIC_GENRE_MAP dictionaries (86% error rate), catch-all fallback dumping platform/community/promo tags into genres, GraphQL queries missing groupName field
Indie genre missing IDF entry — defaulted to 0.10 instead of 0.35, inflating similarity for thousands of Indie-tagged games by 2.2x
OCD Gantt scroll jank: setState on every scroll frame caused full React re-render at 60fps; minimap re-rendered all segment bars every frame; smooth scroll on scrubber queued competing animations
Transmissions archive only searched live news feed — archived items disappeared when feed refreshed; event filter bled into Archived view hiding saved items

Transmission card thumbnails: library_hero.jpg 404s for many games, loading=lazy broken in nested scroll container, images stuck at opacity:0 with no visible fallback, extractImageFromContent captured tracking pixels and relative URLs
Background morphing break on page switch: buzzBgActive two-state timing caused instant header snap from transparent to frosted glass; DarkVeil leaked WebGL contexts on every unmount
days.lookingAhead: Epic catalog needs a full re-sync to ingest groupName-filtered tags. After re-sync, trigger embedding regeneration and ANN rebuild to propagate clean genre signals across the galaxy and neighbourhood system.

days.title: Galaxy Map scales to 111K: streaming projection kills OOM
days.narrative:
Big day of polish and deep stability work. Accent colors unified, ML inference fixed, guided tour realigned, Data Flow rebuilt, Oracle error state redesigned, neighbor connections capped at 0.9 cosine distance.

The headline: the Galaxy Map finally works at scale. With 111K+ embeddings, the Embedding Space was crashing with OOM — the renderer allocated a 456 MB Float32Array for raw 1024D vectors on top of ~1.75 GB baseline memory. Multiple rounds of worker-side fixes (in-place centering, landmark subsampling, mutable refs) helped the worker but couldn't fix the main-thread allocation. The final solution: stream-project each 1024D vector to 100D during the IDB cursor read — L2 normalize, Gaussian random project, store into a pre-allocated 42.5 MB buffer. The full 1024D data never exists in memory. PCA (WebGPU when available, CPU fallback) runs directly on the 100D buffer — fast, deterministic, zero OOM risk. Galaxy builds 111K nodes in seconds at ~1.05 GB renderer memory.

Also shipped: Ollama as optional NSIS installer step, recommendation pipeline 100x speedup, view switching lag eliminated (4 lazy imports + async filter/sort), 11 resource pressure fixes, and crash circuit breaker.
milestones: Galaxy Map builds 111K nodes without OOM — streaming IDB→100D projection eliminates 456 MB main-thread allocation
PCA projection (WebGPU + CPU fallback) replaces UMAP worker — instant, deterministic, no worker hang risk
Random projection preserves pairwise distances (Johnson-Lindenstrauss) — 3D layout quality maintained
Renderer memory stable at ~1.05 GB during galaxy build (was 1.75 GB+, crashing)
Ollama install offered during Ark setup with clear value messaging
Oracle pipeline 100x speedup with dense Float32Array cosine and smart pre-filtering
4 heavy views lazy-imported — 456 KB JS deferred to navigation time
11 resource pressure fixes across RAM, CPU, and GPU
challenges: Main-thread 1024D allocation was the true OOM bottleneck — worker-side fixes alone insufficient
UMAP worker hung silently in Electron renderer despite working in Node.js test — isolated as environment-specific issue
Multiple HMR-triggered concurrent galaxy builds caused resource contention — solved with generation counter and cancellation checks
umap-js O(n²) brute-force fallback with custom distanceFn made 15K landmarks infeasible
days.lookingAhead: Monitor memory in production builds. Consider re-enabling UMAP as an opt-in quality mode once worker communication issues are diagnosed.

days.title: Oracle cross-store fix, snappier Library, Voyage thumbnails restored
days.narrative:
Fixed the ML Recommendation Model always showing 'Not loaded' on both status panels — auto-loads on first status poll with capped retry. Silenced noisy ONNX Runtime warnings during batch inference.

Polished the Embedding Space: the HDR nebula skybox wasn't loading in production (hardcoded '/' path instead of Vite BASE_URL). Smoothed the loading-to-galaxy transition by doubling GPU warmup frames and extending the typewriter lead-in so the scene is fully stable before the overlay fades. Tightened the neighbour limit from 0.9 to 0.7 cosine distance — connections now show genuinely similar games. Fixed the re-queue bug where the galaxy cache was invalidated on entry because the catalog embedding pipeline was still generating vectors — cache now tolerates small count drift when freshly built.

Squashed a recommendation bug where completed games leaked into Oracle shelves via cross-store ID mismatch — candidate pool now checks secondaryId against exclusion set. Library navigation wrapped in startTransition for responsive switching. Voyage thumbnails fixed: TextureLoader CORS mode set to passthrough for Electron file:// context, and cover image memos stabilized on gameId + coverUrl. Embedding Space upgraded with anti-aliasing enabled and bloom resolution doubled from quarter to half.

Finally cracked the HDR skybox — Three.js FileLoader uses fetch() which doesn't support file:// in Electron. Replaced with XHR→Blob URL pipeline and removed the aggressive tex.image nullification that could break texture state.
milestones: ML model auto-loads on first status poll
HDR nebula skybox loads correctly in production builds
Neighbour distance threshold tightened to 0.7 for higher-quality connections
Galaxy cache tolerates in-flight embedding growth — no more re-queue on entry
Completed games no longer leak into Oracle recommendations via cross-store IDs
Voyage thumbnails restored in Electron production builds
Embedding Space anti-aliasing enabled, bloom bumped to half-res
HDR skybox finally loads in production via XHR→Blob URL bypass
challenges: HDR path used absolute '/' which fails with Vite base './'
Galaxy cache strict equality caused unnecessary rebuilds when background pipeline was still running
Cross-store dedup secondaryId not checked during candidate exclusion
THREE.TextureLoader default crossOrigin='anonymous' fails under Electron file:// origin
Three.js FileLoader uses fetch() which doesn't support file:// URLs in Electron production
days.lookingAhead: Monitor neighbor quality with the tighter 0.7 threshold — may need genre-specific tuning for sparse categories.

days.title: Search UX: Searching… until debounce and results
days.narrative: Search UX got a proper pass so the app never flashes 'no games found' before a search has actually run. In browse mode, as soon as you type we now show a 'Searching…' skeleton (spinner + label) until the debounced search finishes — and we treat 'query has results' vs 'query returned empty' only after the request completes, using a completed-query ref so we don't show loading forever when the result is legitimately zero. In library mode, the same idea: if you've typed but the debounced filter hasn't kicked in yet, we show 'Searching…' instead of the full list or an empty state. The search bar spinner now runs in both modes whenever search is pending (raw query ahead of debounce or API in flight). The grid only shows the empty 'no results' state when we're not in that pending state.

days.title: egdata comparison + adult filter reinforced with cuss
days.narrative:
Ran a full exploratory comparison of egdata-api (Epic-only, Hono/MongoDB/Redis) against Tracker — real API calls to api.egdata.app for top-sellers, latest-games, featured, price by country, related offers, achievements, reviews-summary, sales, and autocomplete. Confirmed egdata gives a stable Epic catalog with a real top-sellers list (99 items from a GamePosition-backed collection), regional pricing, related editions/DLC, and optional achievements/IGDB/reviews, while Tracker has no Epic top-sellers list and relies on live GraphQL (plus Cloudflare risk) and REST fallback. Deepened the write-up in docs/egdata-api-comparison.md with architecture, field-level shapes, and integration options.

Then reinforced the adult-content filter so it consistently catches explicit titles. Added the cuss package — a public linguistic list of profane/explicit words with sureness ratings — and use it for word-boundary checks so we don't flag "Assassin" or "classic." Expanded the curated phrase list (e.g. lusty, futa, femboy, hentai, nsfw, sexual content) so titles like "Lusty Bubbles" and "Femboy Futa Mania" are hidden when "Allow adult content" is off. Introduced a cache version so when the filter logic or word list changes we clear the classification cache and re-evaluate every game on next load. All five tests pass; users get a safer default browse without relying on store tags.
milestones: Adult-content filter reinforced with cuss linguistic library and expanded phrase list; cache version invalidates on logic change
days.lookingAhead: Optional: implement egdata as an augmentation source for Epic top sellers and detail enrichment; consider cuss rating threshold tuning if false positives or misses appear.

days.title: Play Streaks layout and 2-decimal value cap
days.narrative: Tightened the Voyage Medals experience so it feels intentional instead of half-empty. The Play Streaks tab now fills the visible area: the tab content uses the same flex layout as Overview, the heatmap lives in a scrollable flex-1 region so the stats bar stays at the top and the grid uses the rest of the space (or scrolls when needed), and the heatmap block is centered so wide screens don't leave dead space on the sides. No more awkward empty bands. Also capped all displayed numeric values to at most two decimal places — added a shared formatMaxTwoDecimals helper and wired it into the Medals view (Taste DNA percentages, genome purity, overview axis labels) so percentages and stats never show runaway precision.
